description: This figure presents engineering tensile stress-strain curves of Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel samples under four different heat treatment conditions: solution-treated (ST, green curve), aged at 550 ℃ for 6 h (550-6, black curve), aged at 600 ℃ for 6 h (600-6, red curve), and aged at 650 ℃ for 6 h (650-6, blue curve). The x-axis denotes engineering strain in %, while the y-axis denotes engineering stress in MPa. All aged samples exhibit significantly higher yield strength and ultimate tensile strength (UTS) but lower ductility compared with the ST sample. The 600-6 sample obtains the highest UTS of ~1100 MPa, followed by 550-6 (~1050 MPa), 650-6 (~970 MPa), and ST (~780 MPa). Elongation decreases from ~72% for ST to ~58% for 550-6 and 600-6, and ~53% for 650-6. These results support that intragranular κ-carbide precipitation delivers effective precipitation strengthening, while severe intergranular κ-carbide precipitation after 650 ℃ aging induces grain boundary weakening and reduces strength.
